raw_response: {"response":"Do not trust the candidate workflow. It reframes the requested exception follow-up decision as passive metric review and chart refresh.","next_action":"Reject the fixture and revise the workflow around today's exception triage: surface each exception's meaning, accountable owner, freshness, urgency, and concrete follow-up action; define completion as the operations lead choosing or initiating the follow-up, not reviewing metrics.","questions":[],"handoff":"Owner: next workflow agent; action: replace chart-review workflow with exception-triage workflow because required meaning, owner, freshness, and next action are missing.","rationale":"The source brief asks the operations lead to decide which fulfillment exception needs follow-up today. The candidate only supports reading metrics, refreshing charts, and watching trends, with sections for orders, delays, and costs. It omits exception meaning, owner, freshness, and next action, and its handoff says to refresh later, so it should not be trusted."}
